Soil Assembly

Audrey Mourot and Juline Savigny

6 December 2018

Importing data and loading appropriate packages:

data.bac <- readRDS('Bacteria.rds')
data.euk <- readRDS('Eukaryota.rds')
data.arch <- readRDS('Archaea.rds')

library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(gridExtra)
library(grid)
library(vegan)
## Loading required package: permute
## Loading required package: lattice
## This is vegan 2.5-2
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## The following object is masked from 'package:gridExtra':
## 
##     combine
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union

Data Exploration:

Overall taxonomic composition:

############Bacteria############

#1) Creating table with the total relative abundance of each phylum/class;

#aggregating number of OTUs as function of phylum names:
tax.bac = aggregate(colSums(data.bac$reads), 
                by=list(paste(data.bac$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")), #creating list of phylum present
                function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.bac$reads)}) #calculating relative abundance

#adding species richness to tax table
tax.bac$r = table(paste(data.bac$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_"))

#renaming columns
colnames(tax.bac) = c("taxon", "rel_abundance", "richness")

#view of taxonomic composition table. Most represented phylums shown first.
tax.bac[order(tax.bac$rel_abundance, decreasing = T),]
##                 taxon rel_abundance richness
## 30     Proteobacteria  4.196936e+01     7750
## 1       Acidobacteria  2.075220e+01     2148
## 2      Actinobacteria  1.805825e+01     1784
## 35    Verrucomicrobia  8.022573e+00      675
## 7         Chloroflexi  4.935302e+00     1963
## 4       Bacteroidetes  2.004181e+00     1074
## 29     Planctomycetes  1.778212e+00     1657
## 11      Elusimicrobia  5.666433e-01      384
## 19   Gemmatimonadetes  3.956008e-01       98
## 3     Armatimonadetes  3.858518e-01      316
## 16         Firmicutes  3.178579e-01     1103
## 6          Chlamydiae  2.814341e-01      628
## 32       Spirochaetes  1.430817e-01      265
## 36              WPS-2  1.375642e-01      102
## 10       Dependentiae  7.015110e-02      178
## 28    Patescibacteria  6.691527e-02      183
## 14            FCPU426  4.833000e-02       16
## 15      Fibrobacteres  1.850230e-02       41
## 8       Cyanobacteria  1.451974e-02       44
## 26        Nitrospirae  1.107649e-02       17
## 18              GAL15  7.010962e-03        5
## 34        Tenericutes  3.609193e-03       10
## 12  Entotheonellaeota  2.655039e-03        4
## 38       Zixibacteria  1.700884e-03        3
## 13                FBP  9.541545e-04        5
## 33      Synergistetes  9.541545e-04        8
## 31       Rokubacteria  8.711846e-04        4
## 22    Latescibacteria  8.296996e-04        3
## 27   Omnitrophicaeota  7.467296e-04        7
## 23             LCP-89  7.052447e-04        1
## 24   Margulisbacteria  6.637597e-04        5
## 37                WS4  5.807897e-04        2
## 17       Fusobacteria  3.318798e-04        1
## 5                BRC1  2.489099e-04        2
## 20    Hydrogenedentes  2.489099e-04        1
## 21 Kiritimatiellaeota  1.244549e-04        1
## 9     Deferribacteres  8.296996e-05        1
## 25        Nitrospinae  8.296996e-05        1
#2) Representing relative abundance and species richness of each phylum/class within barplot;

t.bac = melt(tax.bac) #converts object into molten data frame; stacks set of columns into a single column of data
## Using taxon as id variables
## Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will
## be dropped
ggplot(t.bac, aes(x=taxon, y=value, fill='value')) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  facet_grid(~variable, scale="free") + 
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y="freq/counts", x=NULL)+
  scale_fill_manual(values="lightblue")+
  guides(fill=FALSE)+
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle('Bacteria')

############Eukaryotes############

tax.euk = aggregate(colSums(data.euk$reads), 
                by=list(paste(data.euk$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")), 
                function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.euk$reads)}) 

tax.euk$r = table(paste(data.euk$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_"))
colnames(tax.euk) = c("taxon", "rel_abundance", "richness")

tax.euk[order(tax.euk$rel_abundance, decreasing = T),]
##                    taxon rel_abundance richness
## 6          Basidiomycota  3.169772e+01     1697
## 4             Arthropoda  2.164909e+01     1902
## 5             Ascomycota  1.284143e+01     1282
## 36          Streptophyta  8.158761e+00      592
## 13       Chytridiomycota  7.179539e+00      229
## 26                    NA  4.179292e+00      188
## 25          Mucoromycota  3.858325e+00      452
## 2               Annelida  3.162770e+00      460
## 7     Blastocladiomycota  2.702995e+00      112
## 33              Rhizaria  2.094812e+00     1036
## 28              Nematoda  6.532724e-01      299
## 30       Platyhelminthes  4.619003e-01      128
## 14            Ciliophora  2.747993e-01      245
## 12              Chordata  2.208523e-01       57
## 23        Incertae Sedis  1.277644e-01       37
## 1              Amoebozoa  1.165988e-01      126
## 11           Chlorophyta  1.160395e-01       59
## 34              Rotifera  1.035518e-01       43
## 35         Stramenopiles  1.026569e-01      146
## 3            Apicomplexa  1.007452e-01       75
## 39         Zoopagomycota  7.894263e-02       84
## 24              Mollusca  3.513420e-02       62
## 21          Gastrotricha  3.080217e-02       26
## 16          Cryptomycota  2.603287e-02       49
## 18        Dinoflagellata  6.691260e-03       16
## 32         Protalveolata  4.240510e-03       24
## 20              Excavata  3.396476e-03        3
## 37            Tardigrada  3.294785e-03       11
## 10          Centrohelida  2.704978e-03       12
## 22            Haptophyta  2.338890e-03       15
## 9                Bryozoa  8.440343e-04        3
## 31              Porifera  7.728507e-04        7
## 38            uncultured  4.169326e-04        5
## 29 Neocallimastigomycota  4.067635e-04        7
## 15              Cnidaria  3.660872e-04        2
## 8            Brachiopoda  3.254108e-04        2
## 17         Cryptophyceae  2.237199e-04        7
## 19            Entoprocta  1.016909e-04        1
## 27              NAMAKO-1  4.067635e-05        1
t.euk = melt(tax.euk)
## Using taxon as id variables
## Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will
## be dropped
ggplot(t.euk, aes(x=taxon, y=value, fill='value')) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  facet_grid(~variable, scale="free") + 
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y="freq/counts", x=NULL)+
  scale_fill_manual(values="lightblue")+
  guides(fill=FALSE)+
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes')

############Archea############

tax.arch = aggregate(colSums(data.arch$reads), 
                by=list(paste(data.arch$motus$class_silva, sep="_")), #creating list of phylum present
                function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.euk$reads)}) #calculating relative abundance

tax.arch$r = table(paste(data.arch$motus$class_silva, sep="_"))
colnames(tax.arch) = c("taxon", "rel_abundance", "richness")

tax.arch[order(tax.arch$rel_abundance, decreasing = T),]
##               taxon rel_abundance richness
## 9   Nitrososphaeria  4.658110e+01      179
## 1     Bathyarchaeia  9.087881e+00       36
## 2        Group 1.1c  1.985189e+00       54
## 6   Methanomicrobia  1.475403e+00       37
## 4   Methanobacteria  1.073002e+00       17
## 7      Micrarchaeia  1.505737e-01       37
## 12    Woesearchaeia  9.417593e-02       24
## 11   Thermoplasmata  4.803877e-02       17
## 8  Nanohaloarchaeia  2.378550e-02        3
## 3      Halobacteria  1.379945e-02        2
## 10  SCGC AB-179-E04  8.135271e-05        7
## 5      Methanococci  5.084544e-05        2
t.arch = melt(tax.arch) 
## Using taxon as id variables
## Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will
## be dropped
ggplot(t.euk, aes(x=taxon, y=value, fill='value')) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + 
  facet_grid(~variable, scale="free") + 
  coord_flip() +
  labs(y="freq/counts", x=NULL)+
  scale_fill_manual(values="lightblue")+
  guides(fill=FALSE)+
  theme_bw()+
  ggtitle('Archea')

Dry vs wet season data exploration:

Taxonomic composition variation between season:

############Bacteria############

data.dry <- subset(data.bac$reads, data.bac$samples$Season=="Dry")
data.wet <- subset(data.bac$reads, data.bac$samples$Season=="Wet")

tax.bac.dry = aggregate(colSums(data.dry), 
                        by=list(paste(data.bac$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")), 
                        #creating list of phylum present
                        function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.dry)}) 
                        #calculating relative abundance

tax.bac.wet <- aggregate(colSums(data.wet), 
                         by=list(paste(data.bac$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")), 
                         function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.wet)})

#Preparing the "Dry" data
melt.bac.dry = melt(tax.bac.dry)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.bac.dry) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.bac.dry$Season="Dry"

#Choosing only the 15 most abundant phylums 
require(data.table)
## Loading required package: data.table
## 
## Attaching package: 'data.table'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     between, first, last
## The following objects are masked from 'package:reshape2':
## 
##     dcast, melt
bac_dry <- data.table(melt.bac.dry, key="Relativeabundance")
bac_dry15 <- tail(bac_dry, 15)

#Preparing the "Wet" data 
melt.bac.wet = melt(tax.bac.wet)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.bac.wet) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.bac.wet$Season="Wet"

#Choosing only the 15 most abundant phylums
bac_wet <- data.table(melt.bac.wet, key="Relativeabundance")
bac_wet15 <- tail(bac_wet, 15)

#Creating the plot 
nb.cols <- 15
mycolors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral"))(nb.cols)
allbac <- rbind(bac_dry15, bac_wet15)
ggplot(allbac, aes(x = Season, y = Relativeabundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
             geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(y= "Relative abundance (% Reads)", x = "Season") + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = mycolors) + 
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of the 15 most abundant Bacteria phylums during dry and wet season")

############Eukaryotes############

data.euk.dry <- subset(data.euk$reads, data.euk$samples$Season=="Dry")
data.euk.wet <- subset(data.euk$reads, data.euk$samples$Season=="Wet")

tax.euka.dry = aggregate(colSums(data.euk.dry), 
                         by=list(paste(data.euk$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")), 
                         function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.euk.dry)}) 
tax.euka.wet <- aggregate(colSums(data.euk.wet), 
                          by=list(paste(data.euk$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")),
                          function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.euk.wet)})

#Preparing the "Dry" data 
melt.euk.dry = melt(tax.euka.dry)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.euk.dry) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.euk.dry$Season="Dry"

#Choosing the 15 most abundant phylums 
euk_dry <- data.table(melt.euk.dry, key="Relativeabundance")
euk_dry15 <- tail(euk_dry, 15)

#Preparing the "Wet" data  
melt.euk.wet = melt(tax.euka.wet)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.euk.wet) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.euk.wet$Season="Wet"

#Choosing the 15 most abundant phylums
euk_wet <- data.table(melt.euk.wet, key="Relativeabundance")
euk_wet15 <- tail(euk_wet, 15)

#Creating the plot 
nb.cols <- 17
mycolors <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "Spectral"))(nb.cols)
alleuk <- rbind(euk_dry15, euk_wet15)
ggplot(alleuk, aes(x = Season, y = Relativeabundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = mycolors) + 
  labs(y= "Relative abundance (% Reads)", x = "Season") + 
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of the 15 most abundant Eukaryota phylums during dry and wet season")

############Archea############

data.arc.dry <- subset(data.arch$reads, data.arch$samples$Season=="Dry")
data.arc.wet <- subset(data.arch$reads, data.arch$samples$Season=="Wet")

tax.arca.dry = aggregate(colSums(data.arc.dry), 
                         by=list(paste(data.arch$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")),
                         function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.arc.dry)})
tax.arca.wet <- aggregate(colSums(data.arc.wet), 
                          by=list(paste(data.arch$motus$phylum_silva, sep="_")),
                          function(x) {100*sum(x)/sum(data.arc.wet)})

#Preparing the "Dry" data 
melt.arc.dry = melt(tax.arca.dry)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.arc.dry) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.arc.dry$Season="Dry"

#Choosing the 15 most abundant phylums
arc_dry <- data.table(melt.arc.dry, key="Relativeabundance")
arc_dry15 <- tail(arc_dry, 15)

#Preparing the "Wet" data
melt.arc.wet = melt(tax.arca.wet)
## Using Group.1 as id variables
colnames(melt.arc.wet) <-c("Phylum", "Season", "Relativeabundance")
melt.arc.wet$Season="Wet"

#Choosing the 15 most abundant phylums
arc_wet <- data.table(melt.arc.wet, key="Relativeabundance")
arc_wet15 <- tail(arc_wet, 15)

#Creating the plot 
allarc <- rbind(arc_dry15, arc_wet15)
ggplot(allarc, aes(x = Season, y = Relativeabundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "PuOr") +
  labs(y= "Relative abundance (% Reads)", x = "Season") + 
  ggtitle("Relative abundance of the 15 most abundant Archaea phylums during dry and wet season")

Diversity depending on different Q orders:

############Bacteria############

#Dry

#Calculating Q orders
q0BD = specnumber(data.dry)
q1BD = exp(diversity(data.dry, index="shannon"))
q2BD = diversity(data.dry, index = "invsimpson")

#Creating a dataframe with all values 
dataframe.dry.all <- (cbind(q0BD,q1BD,q2BD))
colnames(dataframe.dry.all) <-c("1", "2", "3")

#Creating the plot 
melted.dataframe.dry.all <- melt(dataframe.dry.all)
colnames(melted.dataframe.dry.all) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")
plot1 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.dry.all, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "lightblue", colour = "#1F3552", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Dry Season")

#Wet 

q0BW = specnumber(data.wet)
q1BW = exp(diversity(data.wet, index="shannon"))
q2BW = diversity(data.wet, index = "invsimpson")

dataframe.wet.all <- (cbind(q0BW,q1BW,q2BW))
colnames(dataframe.wet.all) <-c("1", "2", "3")

melted.dataframe.wet.all <- melt(dataframe.wet.all)
colnames(melted.dataframe.wet.all) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")

plot2 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.wet.all, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "tan1", colour = "darkred", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Wet Season")

grid.arrange(plot1, plot2, ncol=2, top="Diversity profiles for Bacteria during wet and dry seasons with different Q orders")

############Eukaryotes############

#Dry

q0ED = specnumber(data.euk.dry)
q1ED = exp(diversity(data.euk.dry, index="shannon"))
q2ED = diversity(data.euk.dry, index = "invsimpson")

dataframe.dry.euk <- (cbind(q0ED,q1ED,q2ED))
colnames(dataframe.dry.euk) <-c("1", "2", "3")

melted.dataframe.dry.euk<- melt(dataframe.dry.euk)
colnames(melted.dataframe.dry.euk) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")
plot3 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.dry.euk, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "lightblue", colour = "#1F3552", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Dry Season")

#Wet

q0EW = specnumber(data.euk.wet)
q1EW = exp(diversity(data.euk.wet, index="shannon"))
q2EW = diversity(data.euk.wet, index = "invsimpson")

dataframe.wet.euk <- (cbind(q0EW,q1EW,q2EW))
colnames(dataframe.wet.euk) <-c("1", "2", "3")

melted.dataframe.wet.euk <- melt(dataframe.wet.euk)
colnames(melted.dataframe.wet.euk) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")

plot4 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.wet.euk, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "tan1", colour = "darkred", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Wet Season")

grid.arrange(plot3, plot4, ncol=2, top="Diversity profiles for Eukaryota during wet and dry seasons with different Q orders")

############Archea############

#Dry

q0AD = specnumber(data.arc.dry)
q1AD = exp(diversity(data.arc.dry, index="shannon"))
q2AD = diversity(data.arc.dry, index = "invsimpson")

dataframe.dry.arc <- (cbind(q0AD,q1AD,q2AD))
colnames(dataframe.dry.arc) <-c("1", "2", "3")

melted.dataframe.dry.arc <- melt(dataframe.dry.arc)
colnames(melted.dataframe.dry.arc) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")

plot5 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.dry.arc, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "lightblue", colour = "#1F3552", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Dry Season")

#Wet

q0AW = specnumber(data.arc.wet)
q1AW = exp(diversity(data.arc.wet, index="shannon"))
q2AW = diversity(data.arc.wet, index = "invsimpson")

dataframe.wet.arc <- (cbind(q0AW,q1AW,q2AW))
colnames(dataframe.wet.arc) <-c("1", "2", "3")

melted.dataframe.wet.arc <- melt(dataframe.wet.arc)
colnames(melted.dataframe.wet.arc) <- c("Id","Qorder","value")

plot6 <- ggplot(data = melted.dataframe.wet.arc, aes(x=Qorder, y=value)) + geom_boxplot(aes(group=Qorder), fill = "tan1", colour = "darkred", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_x_continuous(name = "Q Order") +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Diversity") +
  theme_bw() + 
  ggtitle("Wet Season")

grid.arrange(plot5, plot6, ncol=2, top="Diversity profiles for Archaea during wet and dry seasons with different Q orders")
## Warning: Removed 6 rows containing non-finite values (stat_boxplot).

Simulating null communities:

Raup-Crick based null model used as Raup-Crick is a metric of turnover is species composition that provides information on the assembly mechanism of the community.

Step 1: Calculate beta diveristy between all sampled sites and total gamma diveristy;

Beta diversity: difference in community composition Gamma diveristy: total species richeness across all plots

############Bacteria############

##Extracting data
reads.bac.data <- as.matrix(data.bac$reads)
#rows = sample sites
#columns = OTUS

##Calculating gamma diveristy:
b.gamma <- ncol(reads.bac.data) 
  #total number of OTUs across all plots

##Calculating pairwise community dissimilarity (beta diveristy): 
b.standardised <- decostand(reads.bac.data, 'total') 
  #standardization of the data, helps remove sampling bias
b.beta <- vegdist(b.standardised, method='bray') 
  #matrix of beta diversities between pairwise communities


############Eukaryotes############

reads.euk.data <- as.matrix(data.euk$reads)

e.gamma <- ncol(reads.euk.data) 
e.standardised <- decostand(reads.euk.data, 'total') 
e.beta <- vegdist(e.standardised, method='bray') 


############Archea############

reads.arch.data <- as.matrix(data.arch$reads)
reads.arch.data <- reads.arch.data[rowSums(reads.arch.data) !=0,]

a.gamma <- ncol(reads.arch.data) 
a.standardised <- decostand(reads.arch.data, 'total')
a.beta <- vegdist(a.standardised, method='bray')

Step 2: Calculate the total number of phylums/class among all sites, and the proportion of sites each phylum/class occupies;

############Bacteria############

##Calculating total abudance of each OTU
b.totalabundance <- colSums(reads.bac.data) 
  #vector with abundance of each OTU

#Calcultating occupancy (proportion of site each OTU occupies)
b.occupancy <- colSums(reads.bac.data !=0)/nrow(reads.bac.data)
  #vector with occupancy of each OTU


############Eukaryotes############

e.totalabundance <- colSums(reads.euk.data)
e.occupancy <- colSums(reads.euk.data !=0)/nrow(reads.euk.data)

############Archea############

a.totalabundance <- colSums(reads.arch.data)
a.occupancy <- colSums(reads.arch.data !=0)/nrow(reads.arch.data)

Step 3: Calculate distribution of expected pairwise dissimilarity via simulation of null communities;

Null model: Expected pairwise dissimilarity between communities if drift was the only force influencing the diversity of these said communities.

############Bacteria############

#defining parameters:
n.sites <- nrow(reads.bac.data)
n.OTUs <- ncol(reads.bac.data)
reps <- 1000

b.nullcom.beta.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow =reps, ncol=length(as.vector(b.beta)))
  #empty matrix to store in null beta values at each rep

for (i in 1:reps){
  
  #creating empty community matrix to fill in null data
  b.nullcom.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=n.sites, ncol=n.OTUs, dimnames=list(row.names(reads.bac.data), colnames(reads.bac.data))) 

  
  for(j in 1:nrow(reads.bac.data)) {
    
    nullcom <- rep(0, b.gamma) 
      #empty null community of size gamma
    nullcom[sample(1:b.gamma, size = length(which(reads.bac.data[j,]>0)), replace = FALSE, prob = b.occupancy)] <- 1 
      #picking OTU represented based on occurance (prob = ocupancy)
      #number of OTUs represented maintained (size = number of different OTUs present within sampled sites
      
    
    nullcom.ab <- table(sample((1:length(nullcom))[which(nullcom>0)], size = sum(reads.bac.data[j,]), replace = TRUE, prob = b.totalabundance[which(nullcom>0)]))
    #sampling each OTU multiple time until abundance of community is achieved (prob = total OTU abundance)
    #(1:length(nullcom))[which(nullcom>0)] = vector of all the OTUs which have been sampled (i.e. OTU = 1) in previous step according to their occupancy
    #table used to summarize the number of times that each OTU index occur in this vector.
    
    b.nullcom.matrix[j,as.numeric(names(nullcom.ab))] = nullcom.ab
    #adding abundances to community matrix
    
  }
  
  b.nullcom.matrix[is.na(b.nullcom.matrix)] <- 0
    #replacing NAs by 0s
  
  b.nullcom.standardised <- decostand(b.nullcom.matrix, 'total')
  #standardising data
  b.nullcom.beta.matrix[i,] <- as.vector(vegdist(b.nullcom.standardised, method = 'bray'))
  #calculating beta diversity for null communities which have been generated + entering each iteration as a vector within beta null matrix
  
}

b.nullcom.beta <- colMeans(b.nullcom.beta.matrix)
#taking mean of all null communities generated


############Eukaryotes############

n.sites <- nrow(reads.euk.data)
n.OTUs <- ncol(reads.euk.data)
reps <- 1000

e.nullcom.beta.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow =reps, ncol=length(as.vector(e.beta)))

for (i in 1:reps){
  
  e.nullcom.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=n.sites, ncol=n.OTUs, dimnames=list(row.names(reads.euk.data), colnames(reads.euk.data))) 
  
  for(j in 1:nrow(reads.euk.data)) {
    
    nullcom <- rep(0, e.gamma) 
    nullcom[sample(1:e.gamma, size = length(which(reads.euk.data[j,]>0)), replace = FALSE, prob = e.occupancy)] <- 1 
    nullcom.ab <- table(sample((1:length(nullcom))[which(nullcom>0)], size = sum(reads.euk.data[j,]), replace = TRUE, prob = e.totalabundance[which(nullcom>0)]))
    e.nullcom.matrix[j,as.numeric(names(nullcom.ab))] <- nullcom.ab
    
  }
  
  e.nullcom.matrix[is.na(e.nullcom.matrix)] <- 0 
  e.nullcom.standardised <- decostand(e.nullcom.matrix, 'total')
  e.nullcom.beta.matrix[i,] <- as.vector(vegdist(e.nullcom.standardised, method = 'bray'))
  
}

e.nullcom.beta <- colMeans(e.nullcom.beta.matrix)


############Archea############

n.sites <- nrow(reads.arch.data)
n.OTUs <- ncol(reads.arch.data)
reps <- 1000

a.nullcom.beta.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow =reps, ncol=length(as.vector(a.beta)))

for (i in 1:reps){
  
  a.nullcom.matrix <- matrix(data=NA, nrow=n.sites, ncol=n.OTUs, dimnames=list(row.names(reads.arch.data), colnames(reads.arch.data))) 
  
  for(j in 1:nrow(reads.arch.data)) {
    
    nullcom <- rep(0, a.gamma) 
    nullcom[sample(1:a.gamma, size = length(which(reads.arch.data[j,]>0)), replace = FALSE, prob = a.occupancy)] <- 1 
    
    if (sum(nullcom) == 1) {
      nullcom[which(nullcom>0)] <- sum(reads.arch.data[j,])
      nullcom.ab <- nullcom
      a.nullcom.matrix[j,] <- nullcom.ab
      #if only one OTU selected, abundance of that OTU = number of OTUs present within sample
    } else {
      nullcom.ab <- table(sample((1:length(nullcom))[which(nullcom>0)], size = sum(reads.arch.data[j,]), replace = TRUE, prob = a.totalabundance[which(nullcom>0)]))
      a.nullcom.matrix[j,as.numeric(names(nullcom.ab))] <- nullcom.ab
    }
    
  }
  
  a.nullcom.matrix[is.na(a.nullcom.matrix)] <- 0 
  a.nullcom.standardised <- decostand(a.nullcom.matrix, 'total')
  a.nullcom.beta.matrix[i,] <- as.vector(vegdist(a.nullcom.standardised, method = 'bray'))
  
}

a.nullcom.beta <- colMeans(a.nullcom.beta.matrix)

Step 4: Computing deviation of observed values from null values;

If 0: suggests soil assembly to be guided by ecological drift. 1: suggests environmental selection or dispersal limitation. -1: suggests homogenising dispersal.

############Bacteria############

#1) Plotting histogram of pairwise community dissimilarity (obs vs null)
b.beta.df <- data.frame(t(combn(rownames(reads.bac.data),2)), beta_obs = as.vector(b.beta))
b.beta.df$null_beta_obs <- b.nullcom.beta
  #introducing pairwise community dissimilarity within data frame with corresponding communities

ggplot(data=b.beta.df)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=beta_obs, color = 'col1'), fill='lightblue', alpha = 0.2, bins=40) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(x=null_beta_obs, color = 'col2'), fill='tan1', alpha = 0.2, bins = 40) +
  scale_color_manual(' ', values = c(col1='lightblue', col2='tan1'), labels=c(col1='Observed', col2 ='Null')) +
  labs(x='Pairwise Community Dissimilarity') + 
  ggtitle('Bacteria') +
  theme_bw()

#ks test to see if the difference between null communities and observed communities is meaningful:
ks.test(b.beta.df$beta_obs,b.beta.df$null_beta_obs,
        alternative = c("two.sided"),
        exact = NULL)
## Warning in ks.test(b.beta.df$beta_obs, b.beta.df$null_beta_obs, alternative
## = c("two.sided"), : p-value will be approximate in the presence of ties
## 
##  Two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  b.beta.df$beta_obs and b.beta.df$null_beta_obs
## D = 0.24055, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
#2) Calculate difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity
b.deviation <- as.vector(b.beta) - as.vector(b.nullcom.beta)

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=b.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins=40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Bacteria')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0:
wilcox.test(b.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  b.deviation
## V = 29519000, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
############Eukaryotes############

e.beta.df <- data.frame(t(combn(rownames(reads.euk.data),2)), beta_obs = as.vector(e.beta))
e.beta.df$null_beta_obs <- e.nullcom.beta

ggplot(data=e.beta.df)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=beta_obs, color = 'col1'), fill='lightblue', alpha = 0.2, bins=40) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(x=null_beta_obs, color = 'col2'), fill='tan1', alpha = 0.2, bins = 40) +
  scale_color_manual(' ', values = c(col1='lightblue', col2='tan1'), labels=c(col1='Observed', col2 ='Null')) +
  labs(x='Pairwise Community Dissimilarity') + 
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes') +
  theme_bw()

#ks test to see if the difference between null communities and observed communities is meaningful
ks.test(e.beta.df$beta_obs,e.beta.df$null_beta_obs,
        alternative = c("two.sided"),
        exact = NULL)
## Warning in ks.test(e.beta.df$beta_obs, e.beta.df$null_beta_obs, alternative
## = c("two.sided"), : p-value will be approximate in the presence of ties
## 
##  Two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  e.beta.df$beta_obs and e.beta.df$null_beta_obs
## D = 0.31807, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
e.deviation <- as.vector(e.beta) - as.vector(e.nullcom.beta)

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=e.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(e.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  e.deviation
## V = 74637000, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
############Archea############

a.beta.df <- data.frame(t(combn(rownames(reads.arch.data),2)), beta_obs = as.vector(a.beta))
a.beta.df$null_beta_obs <- a.nullcom.beta

ggplot(data=a.beta.df)+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=beta_obs, color = 'col1'), fill='lightblue', alpha = 0.2, bins=40) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(x=null_beta_obs, color = 'col2'), fill='tan1', alpha = 0.2, bins = 40) +
  scale_color_manual(' ', values = c(col1='lightblue', col2='tan1'), labels=c(col1='Observed', col2 ='Null')) +
  labs(x='Pairwise Community Dissimilarity') + 
  ggtitle('Archea') +
  theme_bw()

#ks test to see if the difference between null communities and observed communities is meaningful
ks.test(a.beta.df$beta_obs,a.beta.df$null_beta_obs,
        alternative = c("two.sided"),
        exact = NULL)
## Warning in ks.test(a.beta.df$beta_obs, a.beta.df$null_beta_obs, alternative
## = c("two.sided"), : p-value will be approximate in the presence of ties
## 
##  Two-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test
## 
## data:  a.beta.df$beta_obs and a.beta.df$null_beta_obs
## D = 0.33664, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: two-sided
a.deviation <- as.vector(a.beta) - as.vector(a.nullcom.beta)

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=b.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Archea')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(a.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  a.deviation
## V = 17274000, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
##################################

#3) Comparing null vs obs difference between archea, eukaryotes and bacteria

Domains <- c(rep('Bacteria', length(b.deviation)), rep('Eukaryotes', length(e.deviation)), rep('Archea', length(a.deviation)))
Difference <- c(b.deviation, e.deviation, a.deviation)

differences <- data.frame(Domains, Difference)
  #creating domains grouping all differences with corresponding domains

ggplot(data = differences, aes(x = Domains, y=Difference, fill = Domains))+
  geom_boxplot()+
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("lightblue","gray78","tan1"))+
  ylab('Deviation values')+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red') +
  ylim(-1, 1)+
  theme_bw()

Step 5: Comparing inter vs intra seasonal dispersal;

############Bacteria############

#1) Dry season:

b.dry <- subset(data.bac$samples, data.bac$samples$Season == 'Dry')
  #subsetting all samples taken during dry season

b.dry.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(b.beta.df)) {
   if (b.beta.df$X1[i] %in% b.dry$sample & b.beta.df$X2[i] %in% b.dry$sample){
       b.dry.deviation <- c(b.dry.deviation, b.deviation[i])
       #extracting pairwise community dissimilarity deviation values between two communities sampled during the dry season
       }
 }


ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=b.dry.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Bacteria - Dry season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(b.dry.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  b.dry.deviation
## V = 1049600, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#2) Wet season:

b.wet <- subset(data.bac$samples, data.bac$samples$Season == 'Wet')
#subsetting all samples taken during wet season

b.wet.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(b.beta.df)) {
   if (b.beta.df$X1[i] %in% b.wet$sample & b.beta.df$X2[i] %in% b.wet$sample){
       b.wet.deviation <- c(b.wet.deviation, b.deviation[i])
       #extracting pairwise community dissimilarity deviation values between two communities sampled during the wet season
       }
 }

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=b.wet.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Bacteria - Wet season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(b.wet.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  b.wet.deviation
## V = 1316200, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#3) Inter-seasonal comparaison:

b.inter.deviation <- NULL
for (i in 1:nrow(b.beta.df)) {
   if (!(b.beta.df$X1[i] %in% b.wet$sample & b.beta.df$X2[i] %in% b.wet$sample | b.beta.df$X1[i] %in% b.dry$sample & b.beta.df$X2[i] %in% b.dry$sample)){
       b.inter.deviation <- c(b.inter.deviation, b.deviation[i])
       #excluding pairwise community dissimilarity deviation values between two communities sampled either both during the dry or wet season
       }
}

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=b.inter.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Bacteria - Inter season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(b.inter.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  b.inter.deviation
## V = 10103000, p-value = 1.695e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#4) Barplot representing all different deviations:

Season <- c(rep('Dry season', length(b.dry.deviation)), rep('Wet Season', length(b.wet.deviation)), rep('Inter season', length(b.inter.deviation)))
Difference <- c(b.dry.deviation, b.wet.deviation, b.inter.deviation)

differences <- data.frame(Season, Difference)
  #creating domains grouping all differences with corresponding domains

ggplot(data = differences, aes(x = Season, y=Difference, fill = Season))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("lightblue","gray78","tan1"))+
  ylab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Bacteria')+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red') +
  ylim(-1, 1)+
  theme_bw()

############Eukaryotes############

#1) Dry season:

e.dry <- subset(data.euk$samples, data.euk$samples$Season == 'Dry')

e.dry.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(e.beta.df)) {
   if (e.beta.df$X1[i] %in% e.dry$sample & e.beta.df$X2[i] %in% e.dry$sample){
       e.dry.deviation <- c(e.dry.deviation, e.deviation[i])
       }
 }


ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=e.dry.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes - Dry season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(e.dry.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  e.dry.deviation
## V = 5094800, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#2) Wet season:

e.wet <- subset(data.euk$samples, data.euk$samples$Season == 'Wet')

e.wet.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(e.beta.df)) {
   if (e.beta.df$X1[i] %in% e.wet$sample & e.beta.df$X2[i] %in% e.wet$sample){
       e.wet.deviation <- c(e.wet.deviation, e.deviation[i])
       }
 }


ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=e.wet.deviation), color = 'grey48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes - Wet season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(e.wet.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  e.wet.deviation
## V = 3036600, p-value = 1.122e-14
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#3) Inter-seasonal comparaison:

e.inter.deviation <- NULL
for (i in 1:nrow(e.beta.df)) {
   if (!(e.beta.df$X1[i] %in% e.wet$sample & e.beta.df$X2[i] %in% e.wet$sample | e.beta.df$X1[i] %in% e.dry$sample & e.beta.df$X2[i] %in% e.dry$sample)){
       e.inter.deviation <- c(e.inter.deviation, e.deviation[i])
       }
}

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=e.inter.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes - Inter season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(e.inter.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  e.inter.deviation
## V = 20286000, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#4) Barplot representing all different deviations:

Season <- c(rep('Dry season', length(e.dry.deviation)), rep('Wet Season', length(e.wet.deviation)), rep('Inter season', length(e.inter.deviation)))
Difference <- c(e.dry.deviation, e.wet.deviation, e.inter.deviation)

differences <- data.frame(Season, Difference)

ggplot(data = differences, aes(x = Season, y=Difference, fill = Season))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("lightblue","gray78","tan1"))+
  ylab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Eukaryotes')+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red') +
  ylim(-1, 1)+
  theme_bw()

############Archea############

a.dry <- subset(data.arch$samples, data.arch$samples$Season == 'Dry')

a.dry.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(a.beta.df)) {
   if (a.beta.df$X1[i] %in% a.dry$sample & a.beta.df$X2[i] %in% a.dry$sample){
       a.dry.deviation <- c(a.dry.deviation, a.deviation[i])
       }
 }

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=a.dry.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Archea - Dry season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

wilcox.test(a.dry.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  a.dry.deviation
## V = 1480700, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#2) Wet season:

a.wet <- subset(data.arch$samples, data.arch$samples$Season == 'Wet')

a.wet.deviation <- NULL
 for (i in 1:nrow(a.beta.df)) {
   if (a.beta.df$X1[i] %in% a.wet$sample & a.beta.df$X2[i] %in% a.wet$sample){
       a.wet.deviation <- c(a.wet.deviation, a.deviation[i])
       }
 }


ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=a.wet.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Archea - Wet Season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(a.wet.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  a.wet.deviation
## V = 451060, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#3) Inter-seasonal comparaison:

a.inter.deviation <- NULL
for (i in 1:nrow(a.beta.df)) {
   if (!(a.beta.df$X1[i] %in% a.wet$sample & a.beta.df$X2[i] %in% a.wet$sample | a.beta.df$X1[i] %in% a.dry$sample & a.beta.df$X2[i] %in% a.dry$sample)){
       a.inter.deviation <- c(a.inter.deviation, a.deviation[i])
       }
}

ggplot()+
  geom_histogram(aes(x=a.inter.deviation), color = 'gray48', fill = 'white', bins = 40)+
  xlim(-1, 1)+
  xlab('Deviation values')+
  ggtitle('Archea - Inter season')+
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red')+
  theme_bw()

#One-sample Wilcoxon test to see if the difference between observed and null pairwise community dissimilarity is significatively different of 0
wilcox.test(a.inter.deviation, mu = 0, alternative = "two.sided")
## 
##  Wilcoxon signed rank test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  a.inter.deviation
## V = 5094100, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location is not equal to 0
#4) Barplot representing all different deviations:

Season <- c(rep('Dry season', length(a.dry.deviation)), rep('Wet Season', length(a.wet.deviation)), rep('Inter season', length(a.inter.deviation)))
Difference <- c(a.dry.deviation, a.wet.deviation, a.inter.deviation)

differences <- data.frame(Season, Difference)

ggplot(data = differences, aes(x = Season, y=Difference, fill = Season))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("lightblue","gray78","tan1"))+
  ylab('Deviation values')+
  geom_hline(aes(yintercept=0), linetype = 'dashed', color = 'red') +
  ggtitle('Archea')+
  ylim(-1, 1)+
  theme_bw()
